source: Exa crawl - golf ball comparison articles and blogs

quotes.quote: I do not like paying $70+ dollars for a dozen golf balls. That seems too steep of a price for something I will whack around a few rounds and then replace.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Changing Golf Balls To Improve My Game

quotes.quote: I have decided to change my ball from my traditional Pro V1 to a Wilson products. At less than half the price for a dozen golf balls, the Elite 50 and Duo are a good combination for a place to start.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Changing Golf Balls To Improve My Game

quotes.quote: I used the Wilson Duo last Thursday during Men's night and found it to be very suitable. I did not notice any significant loss of distance or feel around the green.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Changing Golf Balls To Improve My Game

quotes.quote: I have tried several different balls over the years and I have always returned to ProV1s – which as you indicated are expensive.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Comments

quotes.quote: I play close to 300 rounds a year so I go through a lot of balls and used balls are so much cheaper.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Comments

quotes.quote: Considering the price and performance, I'd probably still use the Kirkland over the Pro V1. Straightness and spin rates were similar, which are more important in my mind.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: A bit extra distance is nice too, but I don't know if it's worth an extra $2.70 per ball.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: I'm a mid handicapper myself and I think I'd go with the Kirkland balls. I still lose more balls than I'd like, so price plays a big role.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: If you shoot above 90, neither of these balls will be the best choice. The compression rating is a bit high and you might get better performance from something else.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: Waving farewell to a brand new ball as it sails into a water hazard or someone's back garden is all the more painful when you realize that brave, short-lived, dimpled little soldier cost more than $4.50 a pop.
quotes.source: Today's Golfer - Kirkland vs Pro V1 comparison

quotes.quote: What if I told you that you can get a urethane-covered ball that spins more than a Titleist Pro V1… for less than a third of the price?
quotes.source: Today's Golfer - Kirkland Signature V3 comparison

quotes.quote: The main thing that stops people using the best golf balls available is price. Premium balls cost around $55 per dozen, which gets pretty expensive if you tend to lose a few during each round.
quotes.source: Today's Golfer - Golf Ball comparison

quotes.quote: The Kirkland ball is much cheaper, which makes it a solid choice for the average player. It's worth paying more for the Pro V1 if you want the best possible performance.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: The easiest way to see if a ball is premium or value is by looking at the cover. Premium balls have a urethane cover while value balls have an ionomer or surlyn cover.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: You need to ask yourself, can you actually put backspin on the ball? Most people who shoot above 90 can't, not even with the highest-spinning ball.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: The last two years have really helped shape and add weight to the argument that club golfers do not need to be paying top dollar to secure a golf ball.
quotes.source: Today's Golfer - Pro V1 vs DTC comparison

quotes.quote: The biggest gripe about today's golf balls is they're too expensive.
quotes.source: GolfWRX - The cost of golf balls

quotes.quote: When I was a kid, top line golf balls cost $15.00 dollars a dozen. I was making $1.50 an hour minimum wage at my job. So I had to work 10 hours to afford a dozen balls.
quotes.source: GolfWRX - The cost of golf balls

quotes.quote: Value means different things to different people. I am specifically looking for an inexpensive ball that will suit a high-handicapper with a medium swing speed who loses a lot of balls.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Forum - Best value golf ball

quotes.quote: I have come to the realization that, for a high-handicapper, there is little reason to pay a premium for a tour-quality ball.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Forum - Best value golf ball

quotes.quote: My biggest issue is likely inertia. I'm sure there's tons of other great balls out there, but I don't play enough to have the time to fully characterize another ball for how I play.
quotes.source: GolfWRX - For the Amateur is the PROv1 still considered The ball

quotes.quote: And I'd be lying if I said there wasn't some brand loyalty going on too. I grew up around Titleist and I think that's ingrained on my memory.
quotes.source: GolfWRX - For the Amateur is the PROv1 still considered The ball

quotes.quote: I hate paying for golf balls which get damaged or lost easily.
quotes.source: GolfWRX - How much of a difference does a premium ball make

quotes.quote: I typically pick up 10-20 balls per round while I am waiting for a tee to clear: some are super nice Pro V1/TP5X/Tour BXS and a lot are random.
quotes.source: GolfWRX - How much of a difference does a premium ball make

quotes.quote: Kirkland have developed a golf ball that can challenge Tour golf balls for a fraction of the cost.
quotes.source: Today's Golfer - Kirkland Signature 3-Piece V3.0 Review

quotes.quote: When you're shopping for the best golf balls, you might not automatically think about heading to Costco, but I'm going to tell you why the Kirkland Signature 3-Piece V3.0 should be on your radar.
quotes.source: Today's Golfer - Kirkland Signature V3.0 Review

quotes.quote: It seems like every other year or so their 'A' ball is a different name. I swear I couldn't tell you what model was the best ball for Nike, Bridgestone, Callaway, etc.
quotes.source: GolfWRX - For the Amateur is the PROv1 still considered The ball

quotes.quote: Switching golf balls can be daunting, but your approach is logical. Cost-effective choices like Wilson can offer surprising results.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Comments

quotes.quote: If you play the Titleist Pro V1 golf balls, chances are you have had a friend try to tell you that the Kirkland Signature golf ball is the same thing.
quotes.source: Golf Insider UK - Kirkland Golf Ball Review

quotes.quote: I like the duo ball. It's my 2nd choice ball at the moment after the Srixon QStar Tour 5 or 6. The QStar ball seems best for me all around.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Comments

quotes.quote: Yes, that's right. I'm buying used balls, not new. I play close to 300 rounds a year so I go through a lot of balls and used balls are so much cheaper.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Comments

quotes.quote: We've all done it. I'm sure you've hooked or sliced the ball two fairways over before.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: The main difference between premium and value balls is the spin rates around the green.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: Both balls were similar off the tee in terms of straightness. The Pro V1 was longer on average. Both balls had similar results with short-game spin.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: At this point, you might want to consider playing the Pro V1 instead to squeeze a bit more out of your ball. You probably don't lose many balls, so it's fine to pay more.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1

quotes.quote: The challenge for many players is the cost of golf balls in relation to the value received.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Changing Golf Balls

quotes.quote: There is little doubt that we amateurs are overwhelmed by the constant barrage of advertising touting which ball is best for our game.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Changing Golf Balls

quotes.quote: I have tried and gone back to Pro V1 and V1x as well over the years. I think this time that if the Wilson's do not work out, I might try Snell again.
quotes.source: The Grateful Golfer - Comments

quotes.quote: I've seen a few good golfers say they also liked the Kirkland ball.
quotes.source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland vs Pro V1
